weekly.title: VN-Index Breaks 1,825 Support on WTI Spike, DXY Reset; Tech Holds the Line at +3.88%
weekly.executive_summary: VN-Index surrendered early-week gains, breaking below the 1,825 support flagged in our Q2-2026 framework to close at 1,838.90 (-1.53% w/w) after printing an intraday low of 1,798.05. WTI's pullback to USD 90.54 from elevated levels failed to cushion Oil & Gas (-3.41%), while Technology extended leadership at +3.88% on continued VinFast and FPT tailwinds. DXY collapsed to 100.07 yet USD/VND firmed to 26,275 — a divergence that demands attention. Foreign investors were net sellers of VND 6,688.85bn for the week, with one large sell day on 4 June driving most of the pressure. Tone: defensive; risk-reward shifting toward the 1,775/1,800 band.

monthly.vn_index_trend: Defensive month in May 2026. Monthly low at 1,840 established as critical support, with May highs near 1,900 now acting as resistance. A clean break below 1,840 opens the path toward 1,800; a reclaim of 1,900 would re-establish the May highs as resistance-turned-support. Net positioning into June: neutral with a slight defensive tilt.
key_themes: Global liquidity conditions stable, but growth-sensitive assets pricing in softening demand (WTI -14.30%, BTC -5.88%)
Domestic liquidity structurally elevated at ~VND 26tn/day versus 2024-25 averages
VND peg intact; SBV stable-FX posture absorbs DXY bid (DXY +0.71% to 98.91) without yield-stress spillover
Mid-month MSCI rebalance window and Q2 earnings pre-announcement cycle (Banking, Real Estate) as principal June catalysts
Retail sector -9.17% May drawdown flagged as leading indicator for soft May consumer-spend data
Gold (-1.50% to 4,560.50) gave back portion of prior-month premium but remains a tail-risk hedge of choice
monthly.macro_regime: Stable global liquidity overlaying a softening global growth signal. Domestic offset from VND stability, intact onshore liquidity, and SBV's defensive-FX posture preserve the carry argument for foreign allocators. External backdrop is the principal risk channel; domestic fundamentals remain supportive. Stance: neutral with a slight defensive tilt.
monthly.foreign_flow_direction: Neutral with a cautiously constructive lean. Intact carry argument, stable VND, and the upcoming MSCI rebalance window support an inflow bias, but the soft global growth signal and one-sided FX risk (DXY pushing toward 99 with no VND response) cap conviction. Commentary does not confirm explicit May flow data.
forward_risks: WTI -14.30% contagion into Energy-sector earnings revisions, particularly for upstream-exposed names
Soft May consumer-spend data extending the Retail -9.17% de-rating
DXY break higher toward 99+ forcing a defensive SBV response and one-sided FX-channel stress
VN-Index break of the 1,840 monthly support opening a path to 1,800

quarterly.vn_index_trend: VN-Index broke the 1,825 support floor flagged in the Q2-2026 framework, closing at 1,838.90 (-1.53% w/w) after a volatile week that opened at 1,867.44 and printed a 1,871.09 high before capitulating to an intraday low of 1,798.05. The Thursday breakdown candle breached 1,825 and tagged 1,798.05 on closing-auction pressure; Friday's 1,838.90 close represents a tactical 40-point dead-cat bounce off the low but failed to reclaim the 1,850 pivot. A wide 73-point weekly range (1,798.05-1,871.09) replaced last week's indecisive near-doji with a decisive bearish-engulfing structure, shifting risk-reward toward the 1,775/1,800 band. Breadth deteriorated: 6 of 9 tracked sectors closed red, with cyclicals (Real Estate -3.18%, Oil & Gas -3.41%, Securities -1.82%) leading the decline and Technology (+3.88%), Construction (+2.11%), and Retail (+1.96%) the only green. Foreign investors were net sellers of VND 6,688.85bn for the week, including a large 4 June outflow. Tone: defensive, with rotation from cyclicals into growth.
key_themes: Vietnam tech re-rating continues: Technology led the tape at +3.88% on VinFast/FPT tailwinds, now extending into broader ICT narrative despite index drawdown
Power-sector governance overhang persists: PV Gas/EVN ecosystem role remains in flux following last week's detention event; Utilities drag extends
WTI regime shift: oil spiked from USD 79.36 to 90.54, compressing refiner crack spreads and weighing on Oil & Gas (-3.41%)
DXY capitulation to 100.07 (from 104.33) failed to transmit to VND: USD/VND firmed to 26,275 (+3% vs. last reference), indicating SBV-managed depreciation for export cushion
VND decoupling from DXY: SBV tolerating weaker VND despite USD weakness, deliberate policy to absorb post-EV-incentive current-account dynamic and WTI shock
EV incentive regime continues to support VinFast volume ramp: 50% registration-fee waiver through 2030 still in force
EVN balance-sheet repair: ~150,000 bn VND deposit redeployment continues to defuse SOE credit-complex drag
Gold safe-haven bid unwinding: -3.10% w/w to 4,337.10 reduces defensive allocation support for EM portfolios
BTC weakness (-4.51% to 60,922.67) pressures VinFast/FPT retail-investor base and risk-asset correlation channel
Foreign-flow rotation: directional bias from cyclicals into Tech aligns with post-VinFast-capital-raise narrative
Liquidity normalized across the quarter at ~15,000-22,000 bn VND/day (USD ~580-850m), consistent with HOSE historical range using corrected traded-value units
quarterly.macro_environment: SBV posture shifted to managed depreciation: USD/VND firmed to 26,275 (-0.06% w/w) but is now ~3% above the 25,499 reference, with DXY collapsing from 104.33 to 100.07 yet VND not strengthening — clear decoupling indicating SBV is using the band for export cushion and current-account absorption rather than appreciation. DXY sub-101 print is the year's most significant USD move, reflecting Fed expectations reset and term-premium compression. WTI at USD 90.54 caps net-importer inflation risk while pressuring downstream refiners (PLX/BSR) via lagged administrative pump-price passthroughs. Gold at 4,337.10 and BTC at 60,922.67 both declined, signaling risk-on rotation out of defensive hedges. Corporate-side catalysts from Q2-2026 week 1 (EVN deposit redeployment, EV fee-waiver extension) continue to defuse structural drags, but the WTI spike and DXY/VND divergence forced macro back into the driver's seat this week. Overall: stable FX regime with deliberate managed weakness, contained inflation, but increased two-sided risk from cross-asset volatility.

technical_levels.key_moving_averages: MA20 to be formally calibrated next week with a second weekly close; current 1,838.90 print sets the initial base. Weekly structure shifted from near-doji to bearish-engulfing.

forward_risks: Sustained foreign selling breaking 1,798 support toward 1,775/1,750 (200-day region)
WTI spike above USD 95 forcing SBV policy response and reigniting inflation complex
DXY mean-reversion above 101 forcing sharper VND band test given current 26,275 level
VND decoupling stress if SBV widens band unexpectedly or DXY reverses sharply
Second governance event in power complex extending Utilities weakness beyond current drag
Refiner margin compression persisting (PLX/BSR crack-spread pressure) if WTI stays above 90
Upstream services capex delay risk for PVD/PVS if WTI fails to hold above 90
BTC weakness deepening weighing on VinFast/FPT retail-investor base and risk-asset correlation
Gold safe-haven unwinding freeing flows for either risk-on re-engagement or further EM de-risking
Liquidity running ~15,000-22,000 bn VND/day in line with Q2-2026 baseline using corrected traded-value units
Foreign-flow pressure confirmed by reported buy/sell values: -6,688.85 bn VND net for the week ending 5 June
Idiosyncratic catalysts (margin calls, regulatory actions) layering on macro tape and amplifying two-sided risk

continuity_rules: Weekly notes should update, not reset, the active monthly and quarterly narrative.
Monthly notes should reconcile the weekly tape and preserve the quarter-level thesis.
Quarterly memory is the long-term anchor for levels, risks, and sector rotation.
Contradictions between weekly, monthly, and quarterly views must be explicitly resolved.
